free_text: Study of activation cross sections of proton induced
reactions on erbium for practical applications
Stack1 36 MeV
Stack2 70 MeV
High purity Er foils (Goodfellow),
thicknesses: 30 and 40 micron.
Incident proton energy: 36 and 70 MeV.
Nondestructive measurement.
Counting was started about 2-3 hours after end of
bombardment (EOB) at VUB and one day after EOB
at CYRIC.
The excitation functions were determined in an accurate
way relative to the monitor reactions.
Ti (12 and 30 micron) and Al (100 micron) foils.
The monitor foils were inserted evenly in the
irradiated target stack to re-measure the complete
excitation function of the monitor reaction over the
investigated energy range.
Chapter 4
Volume 3
The energy degradation along the stack was determined
via calculation and corrected on the basis of the
simultaneously re-measured monitor reactions.
Decay data were taken from NuDat2 database.
Not detailed.
Not detailed.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
The measured experimental data were compared to
and analyzed with theoretical calculations based on
the model code ALICE-IPPE.
On the basis of the new experimental cross
sections integral yields were calculated.
KB
To determine the activity of the 170Tm and to avoid any
overlapping from the 84.94 keV Kb1 X-ray line from
gamma-ray excitation of lead (used in shielding
material), customized measurements with an X-ray
detector in air, far from any lead material were made.
In spite of these precautions the X-ray of lead still
appears in the spectra but with very low intensity.
This was corrected on the basis of the well separated
74.97 keV Ka1 X-ray line of Pb.
The resulting excitation function consequently has
larger uncertainties.
Data were taken from Table 2 of the Ref.
